title: in-language test/expect blocks + assertion vocabulary
manager: Tests written in FSL itself — `test`/`expect` blocks with a rich assertion vocabulary (reaches, blocked, invariant-holds, temporal, IO-relation, replay-equivalence, coverage) — so a machine's tests live with the machine and codegen-export alongside it. This collapses the gap between the machine and its tests: an assertion can itself be a model-check, and the tests travel wherever the machine is compiled instead of being stranded in a separate host harness.
cs: in-language `test`/`expect` with assertions (reaches/blocked/invariant-holds/temporal/IO-relation/replay-equivalence/coverage); codegen-exported; tests live with the machine.
